name: rust-studio
description: Turn a single Claude Code session into a full Rust engineering studio. Tiered agent team (architect -> leads -> specialists), path-scoped Rust standards, quality gates, and cargo-aware hooks across libraries, async/web services, CLIs, and systems/embedded code.

test_runner.title: Default test runner
test_runner.description: Test runner the studio prefers (cargo-nextest recommended). Surfaced in the session briefing so agents honor it.

gate_intensity.title: Default gate intensity
gate_intensity.description: Default rigor for quality gates and reviews: full, lean, or solo. Surfaced in the session briefing.

default_msrv.title: House MSRV fallback
default_msrv.description: Minimum supported Rust version to assume when a Cargo.toml does not declare rust-version (e.g. 1.82). Leave empty to keep showing "(unset)".

memory_recall.title: Session-start memory recall
memory_recall.description: Scan the Obsidian vault at session start and inject a ranked index of relevant project notes. Turn off to skip the vault scan; the /remember, /recall, and /session-wrap skills still work.

vault_path.title: Obsidian vault path
vault_path.description: Path to the Obsidian vault used for cross-session memory. Overrides the OBSIDIAN_VAULT_PATH env var. Leave empty to use OBSIDIAN_VAULT_PATH, or ~/memory by default.

auto_capture.title: Auto-capture learnings
auto_capture.description: When a turn finishes a real unit of work (a completion summary + uncommitted changes) but nothing was saved to memory, block the stop ONCE and nudge the agent to /remember any non-obvious, durable learning. The agent does the judging and writing (a hook has no MCP access). Nudges at most once per turn and never re-blocks (stop_hook_active breaks the loop), so it is far gentler than stop_guard. Turn off to keep memory capture purely manual.

statusline.title: Auto-install status line
statusline.description: On the first session, install the rich studio status line into ~/.claude/settings.json (a plugin cannot ship a top-level statusLine itself). It is installed ONCE, never clobbers an existing statusLine, and backs settings up first. Turn off to skip auto-install; manage or remove anytime with /progress-bar.

progress_tracking.title: Progress visibility
progress_tracking.description: When on, orchestrating skills like /dev-task keep a live task list (one task per phase, ticking scout → plan → build → review) and surface each phase's result as it completes — so you follow progress on the task list instead of waiting in silence until the end. Turn off for terse runs.

routing_nudge.title: Routing nudge
routing_nudge.description: Once per session, remind you to prefer a studio skill and to /recall an area before working. Turn off to silence the UserPromptSubmit nudge.

fmt_nudge.title: Formatting nudge
fmt_nudge.description: After a turn that changed .rs files, nudge to run /lint when they are not rustfmt-clean. Turn off to silence the Stop-hook fmt check.

stop_guard.title: Stop-guard (block undisciplined turn endings)
stop_guard.description: When on, block the turn from ending if the final message dodges ownership, seeks permission, stops early, avoids tests, leaves stubs, hands the work back to you, or claims done without evidence. The feedback is fed back to the model so it continues. Aggressive (it can block legitimate stops) — off by default; the mechanical teeth for docs/integrity-and-evidence.md.

stop_guard_strict.title: Stop-guard: strict mode
stop_guard_strict.description: Also block on soft signals (speculation, weak completion, evidence-free claims) even when some completion evidence is present. Requires stop_guard on.

stop_guard_require_evidence.title: Stop-guard: require evidence
stop_guard_require_evidence.description: Block any turn ending whose final message lacks concrete completion evidence (files changed / commands run / verification / result), even with no flagged phrase. Requires stop_guard on.
